questions.text: You are told that Country A experienced growth of real GDP per person of 4 percent per year throughout the 1900s. In view of other countries’ experience, you would have to characterize Country A’s growth as

options.text: exceptionally high.
moderately high.
moderately low.
exceptionally low.

questions.text: The level of real GDP person

options.text: differs widely across countries, but the growth rate of real GDP per person is similar across countries.
is very similar across countries, but the growth rate of real GDP per person differs widely across countries.
and the growth rate of real GDP per person are similar across countries.
and the growth rate of real GDP per person vary widely across countries.

questions.text: Which of the following countries had the highest growth rate over the last 100 or so years?

options.text: Brazil
Germany
Canada
United States

questions.text: Which of the following countries had the highest level of real GDP per person in 2006?

options.text: Germany
United Kingdom
United States
Japan

questions.text: The average amount of goods and services produced from each hour of a worker's time is called

options.text: GDP.
per capita GDP.
productivity.
technological knowledge.

questions.text: Dilbert’s Incorporated produced 5,000,000 units of accounting software in 2004. At the start of 2005 the pointy-haired boss reduced total annual hours of employment from 10,000 to 8,000 and production was 4,800,000. These numbers indicate that productivity

options.text: fell by 4%.
fell by 20%.
rose by 12%.
rose by 20%.

questions.text: Dilbert’s Incorporated produced 6,000,000 units of software in 2005. At the start of 2006 the pointy- haired boss raised employment from 10,000 total annual hours to 14,000 annual hours and production was 7,000,000 units. Based on these numbers what happened to productivity?

options.text: It fell by about 16.7%.
It stayed the same.
It rose by about 16.7%.
It rose by about 40%.

questions.text: Which of the following is human capital?

options.text: textbooks
hand held power tools
understanding how to repair cars
All of the above are correct.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is true?

options.text: Natural resources per worker influence productivity only when those natural resources are renewable.
The prices of most natural resources are stable or falling relative to other prices.
Technology requires greater use of natural resources.
The terms human capital and technological knowledge are used interchangeably.

questions.text: Suppose over the last year that the price of iron ore increased from $1,200 a ton to $1,300 a ton. Over the same time a measure of the overall price level increased from 168 to 187. The price of iron ore increased by

options.text: less than inflation, and this means it became relatively less scarce.
less than inflation, and this means it became scarcer.
more than inflation, and this means it became scarcer.
more than inflation, but this doesn’t necessarily mean that it become scarcer.
the same thing as technological knowledge.
the same thing as labor.
the tools and equipment operated by humans.
knowledge and skills that workers have acquired.

questions.text: If there are constant returns to scale, the production function can be written as

options.text: xY = 2xAF(L, K, H, N).
Y/L = A F(xL, xK, xH, xN).
Y/L = A F( 1, K/L, H/L, N/L).
L = AF(Y, K, H, N). Scenario 25-1. An economy’s production form takes the form Y = AF(L, K, H, N).

questions.text: Refer to Scenario 25-1. In the production function, which variable would increase in value as technology improved?

options.text: A
K
H
N

questions.text: Refer to Scenario 25-1. If the production function has the constant-returns-to-scale property, then if we know the values of A, K/L, H/L, and N/L, we also know the value of

options.text: output.
labor productivity.
A.
All of the above are correct.

questions.text: Consider three imaginary countries. In Old York, saving amounts to $3,000 and consumption amounts to $7,000; in New Frank, saving amounts to $2,000 and consumption amounts to $8,000; and in Ganzee, saving amounts to $4,500 and consumption amounts to $10,500. The saving rate is

options.text: higher in Old York than in Ganzee, and it is higher in Ganzee than in New Frank.
higher in New Frank than in Ganzee, and it is higher in Ganzee than in Old York.
higher in Ganzee than in New Frank, and it is the same in New Frank and Old York.
higher in Old York than in New Frank, and it is the same in Old York and Ganzee.
South Korea had a higher growth rate than the United States because it had a higher ratio of investment to GDP.
the United States had a higher growth rate than South Korea because it had a higher ratio of investment to GDP.
South Korea had a higher growth rate than the United States even though it had a similar ratio of investment to GDP.
the United States had a higher growth rate than South Korea even though it had a similar ratio of investment to real GDP.

questions.text: All else equal, if there are diminishing returns, then which of the following is true if a country increases its capital by one unit?

options.text: Output will rise by more than it did when the previous unit was added.
Output will rise but by less than it did when the previous unit was added.
Output will fall by more than it did when the previous unit was added.
Output will fall but by less then it did when the previous unit was added.

questions.text: All else equal, if there are diminishing returns, then if a country raised its capital by 100 units last year and by 100 units this year,

options.text: the increase in output was greater for this year than last year.
the increase in output was greater last year than this year.
the increase in output is the same in both years.
None of the above is necessarily correct.
